steps.title: Identify the root cause of the myth's persistence
steps.content: Text 2 directly states the motivation: “Our belief that the coming of film was transformative is so strong that we invented and keep retelling a founding myth.” The signal is clear — the story persists not due to factual evidence but because of our perception about film's significance.

steps.title: Match the signal to the correct answer
steps.content:
C directly articulates this: the description is “more motivated by a perception of the significance of the invention of film than by facts.” The other three either mischaracterize the myth's structure (A), reverse historians' actual stance (B), or treat the myth as factually grounded rather than invented (D).

This matches choice C — C is correct.
